---
title: "treetools"
author: "Alexander Shah"
format: html
---

## Setup

```{r}
#| include: false
install.packages("rlang", repos = "https://cloud.r-project.org")
install.packages("dplR", repos = "https://cloud.r-project.org")
install.packages("tidyr", repos = "https://cloud.r-project.org")
install.packages("dplyr", repos = "https://cloud.r-project.org")
library(dplR)
library(tidyr)
library(dplyr)
```

## Functions

```{r}
# Reads a RWL file and returns a data frame
read_rwl <- function(path) {
  read.rwl(path)
}

# Reshapes the wide data frame into a long tidy format
tidy_rwl <- function(rwl_df) {
  # The row names of the RWL data frame are the years,
  # pull them out as a column
  rwl_df$year <- as.integer(rownames(rwl_df))
  # Pivot from wide to long format
  tidy <- pivot_longer(rwl_df, cols = -year, names_to = "sample_id", values_to = "ring_width")
  # Convert back to a plain data frame
  tidy <- as.data.frame(tidy)
  # Drop rows where ring width is NA
  tidy <- tidy[!is.na(tidy$ring_width), ]
  tidy
}

# Saves the tidy data frame to a CSV file
export_rwl <- function(data, path) {
  write.csv(data, path, row.names = FALSE)
  invisible(path)
}

# Flags statistical outliers (Z-score > 3) as potential cross-dating errors
detect_crossdate_errors <- function(data) {
  # Calculate Z-score for each measurement within its sample
  z_scores <- ave(data$ring_width, data$sample_id, FUN = function(x) {
    (x - mean(x)) / sd(x)
  })
  # Flag rows where Z-score exceeds 3
  outliers <- data[abs(z_scores) > 3, ]
  outliers$issue <- "Statistical outlier (Z > 3)"
  outliers
}
```

## Test

```{r}
# Load a sample dataset built into dplR
data(gp.rwl, package = "dplR")

# Run the pipeline
tidy_data <- tidy_rwl(gp.rwl)

# Check output
head(tidy_data)
```

```{r}
# Export to CSV
export_rwl(tidy_data, "cores_output.csv")
```

```{r}
# Test error detection on tidy data
detect_crossdate_errors(tidy_data)
```
